sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: A new academic study (1) ...... (HIGH) the (2) ...... (EXTEND) to which student loan debt is shaping career choices among graduates.
Responsibility is (3) ...... (PORTION) unfairly to universities alone, which, critics argue, will go to any (4) ...... (LONG) to recruit international applicants.
(5) ...... (ADD), the findings reveal that many graduates delay major life decisions due to the pressure of outstanding loans.
A controversial funding proposal — which has since been (6) ...... (DRAW) — sought to double tuition fees for all postgraduate programmes.
While the financial burden placed on graduates is not (7) ...... (CONSIDER), it would be misleading to claim that every degree leads to hardship.
The (8) ...... (MAJOR) of respondents admitted they had fallen behind on their (9) ...... (PAY) within the first two years of leaving university.
There will always be a (10) ...... (MINOR) of borrowers who genuinely struggle to manage their finances effectively.

sections.title: B: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
Sending those aggressive emails to a client like that is bound to cause you serious problems. **ICE**
You are ....... by sending those aggressive emails to a client like that.

Are you telling me that Rachel gets irritated very easily, Tom? **SHORT**
Are you telling me that Rachel ........ , Tom?

There’s no doubt that comparing prices online is a worthwhile thing to do. **PAYS**
There’s no doubt ...... prices online.

I’d prefer to use every single facility at the spa for the entire afternoon. **WORTH**
I’d rather I ...... by using every single facility at the spa for the entire afternoon.

I think Marcus is preoccupied with several worries right now. **MIND**
I think Marcus ...... right now.
items.answer: has a lot on his mind

items.sentence:
Why didn’t you look everywhere for your passport? You might have caught the flight. **LOW**
If ...... for your passport, you might have caught the flight.
items.answer: you had searched high and low

items.sentence:
I can’t manage at all with this advanced statistics module, and it worries me. **DEPTH**
I wish ...... this advanced statistics module.
items.answer: I was not out of my depth in

items.sentence:
Kevin was the unlucky one so he had to inform Claire that she was being transferred to another branch. **STRAW**
If Kevin ......, he wouldn’t have had to inform Claire that she was being transferred to another branch.
items.answer: had not drawn the short straw

sections.title: C: Collocations (Financial Terms)
sections.instructions: Write a word from the box in each gap. There is one word you will not use.

items.sentence: After thirty years, they finally paid off the ...... on their seaside cottage. Now it’s truly theirs!
All our household bills go out by direct ......, which saves us the hassle of remembering due dates.
We agreed to buy the furniture in instalments, so we made the first down ...... this afternoon.
Upon retirement, you may choose to receive a single lump ...... or opt for monthly instalments spread over fifteen years.
After two decades as a nurse, she now receives a generous state ...... that comfortably covers her living costs.
Since I always run out of money before payday, I’ve decided to apply for a/an ...... facility at my bank.
The letting agency requires a 20% ...... before handing over the keys to the apartment.
The court ordered the company to pay Mr Rivera £250,000 ...... for the injuries he sustained at work.
If we all **gather / club / pool** together, we can easily cover the cost of a weekend away.
You’ll need to **stir / drum / beat** up a lot more enthusiasm if you want the committee to back your proposal.
The veteran pilot had **logged / clocked / counted** up over 5,000 hours of commercial flying time.
Our renovation costs are **rising / growing / mounting** up and we’re still only halfway through the project.
Let’s **weigh / measure / size** the options up carefully before we commit to signing the contract.
It seems like a large sum, but if you **split / break / divide** it down into weekly amounts, it’s quite manageable.
She agreed to lend him the cash, but insisted he **give / pay / return** her back before the month was out.
Investors have been **snapping / buying / grabbing** up properties along the new transport corridor.
It’s high time ...... more seriously about your future career path.
I’d rather you ...... anything to your parents yet, but I realise it’s difficult to keep it quiet.
Do you wish ...... an official complaint about the quality of the service?
If only people from our parents’ generation ...... the level of global travel that we enjoy today.
Sophie definitely wishes she ...... to prepare a full financial report by tomorrow morning.
...... any slower, we’d never have caught the last train home.
I’d never lend money to a complete stranger unless they ...... me proper identification.
Imagine email ...... invented. Communication would be so much slower, wouldn’t it?
